name: cssdoc-vscode
description: VS Code support for cssdoc: completions, hover, go-to-definition, and deprecation quick-fixes for documented CSS classes, modifiers, and custom properties.

scripts.package: vp run -t build && vsce package --no-dependencies -o cssdoc.vsix

cssdoc.include.markdownDescription: Globs for the documented CSS the language server reads. Auto-detects all CSS in the workspace by default.

cssdoc.css.markdownDescription: Explicit paths to documented CSS files. When set, this overrides `#cssdoc.include#` / `#cssdoc.exclude#` auto-detection.
cssdoc.css.markdownDeprecationMessage: Prefer `#cssdoc.include#` / `#cssdoc.exclude#`. This explicit list still works and takes precedence when non-empty.

enumDescriptions: Header, summary, deprecation, and a facet count line.
Every section that has content, each listed with its description (empty sections are omitted).
cssdoc.hover.detail.markdownDescription: How much detail a documented component's hover card shows. Use `custom` to control each section via `#cssdoc.hover.sections#`.

cssdoc.hover.sections.markdownDescription: Per-section visibility when `#cssdoc.hover.detail#` is `custom`. Each value is `auto` (show only when the section has content — what `full` does), `on` (always show; empty renders a placeholder), or `off` (hide). Keys: `summary`, `deprecated`, `remarks`, `accessibility`, `modifiers`, `parts`, `shadowParts`, `states`, `customProperties`, `functions`, `slots`, `animations`, `layers`, `conditions`, `see`, `structure`, `examples`. Unlisted sections default to `auto`.

cssdoc.hover.sectionOrder.markdownDescription: The order sections appear in a component's hover card. List section keys in the order you want; any section you omit is dropped from the card. Leave empty to use the default order. The header (name, kind, release stage, since) always leads.
